 {"id":"bd-0zp7","title":"Add missing hook calls in mail reply and ack","description":"The mail commands are missing hook calls:

1. runMailReply (mail.go:525-672) creates a message but doesn't call hookRunner.Run(hooks.EventMessage, ...) after creating the reply in direct mode (around line 640)

2. runMailAck (mail.go:432-523) closes messages but doesn't call hookRunner.Run(hooks.EventClose, ...) after closing each message (around line 487 for daemon mode, 493 for direct mode)

This means GGT hooks won't fire for replies or message acknowledgments.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T20:52:53.069412-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}

3. Retry nudge if no response within N seconds

 {"id":"bd-hobo","title":"Distinct prefixes for protos, molecules, wisps","description":"Template/workflow entities should have visually distinct prefixes from regular issues.

**Problem:**
- Protos (bd-7bs4) look like regular issues - invites squashing
- Molecules (poured instances) also use bd- prefix
- Wisps are in separate DB but still use bd- when referenced

**Proposed Prefixes:**
- `proto-` for templates (e.g., proto-release, proto-review)
- `mol-` for active molecules (poured from protos)
- `wisp-` for ephemeral wisps (vapor phase)

**Benefits:**
- Instant visual recognition of entity type
- Prevents accidental modification of templates
- Clear lifecycle: proto → mol → wisp → digest

**Implementation options:**
1. Separate databases with different prefixes
2. Issue type determines prefix generation
3. Naming convention enforced by bd pour/wisp commands","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-24T16:45:24.940809-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T02:04:52.459233-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T02:04:52.459233-08:00","labels":["workflow"]}
+{"id":"bd-hp8g","title":"gt sling should look up formulas from .beads/formulas/ registry","description":"## Problem

gt sling <formula-name> doesn't find formulas from the registry that bd formula list shows.

## What I tried

1. gt sling beads/crew/emma --formula beads-release --var version=0.42.0
   - Problem: --formula flag doesn't exist

2. gt sling beads-release beads/crew/emma --var version=0.42.0
   - Error: open .../beads-release: no such file or directory
   - Problem: Looks for file in cwd, not formula registry

## What worked

bd mol wisp beads-release --var version=0.42.0 - correctly finds formula from .beads/formulas/

## Expected

gt sling should use the same formula lookup as bd mol wisp - checking .beads/formulas/*.formula.json

## Documentation gap

The help shows examples like 'gt sling mol-release mayor/' suggesting formula slinging works, but it doesn't find registry formulas.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"beads/polecats/jade","created_at":"2025-12-30T21:18:17.251292-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-30T22:06:27.153176-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T22:06:27.153176-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed in both repos: bd cook now looks up formulas from registry, and gt sling uses bd mol wisp"}
 {"id":"bd-hr39","title":"bd cook: needs field not converted to step dependencies","description":"When cooking a formula with `needs` fields on steps, the dependencies are not created between sibling steps.

## Expected

Formula:
```yaml
steps:
  - id: inbox-check
    title: Check inbox
  - id: trigger-spawns
    title: Nudge polecats
    needs: [inbox-check]
```

Should create:
- mol-foo.inbox-check (no deps)
- mol-foo.trigger-spawns → depends on → mol-foo.inbox-check

## Actual

Both steps only depend on the parent proto:
- mol-foo.inbox-check → depends on → mol-foo
- mol-foo.trigger-spawns → depends on → mol-foo

The `needs` field is ignored.

## Impact

This breaks the step execution order. Steps that should wait for predecessors will run in parallel or out of order.

## Reproduction

```bash
bd cook mol-deacon-patrol.formula.yaml
bd show mol-deacon-patrol.trigger-pending-spawns
# Shows: Depends on mol-deacon-patrol (parent only)
# Should show: Depends on mol-deacon-patrol.inbox-check
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-24T13:49:59.023514-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T13:59:09.929298-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T13:59:09.929298-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-hulf","title":"Molecule execution state management","description":"Implement molecule execution state tracking.

## State Location

```
.beads/molecules/<mol-id>.state.yaml
```

## State Schema

```yaml
id: mol-deacon-patrol
formula: mol-deacon-patrol  # Source formula
bonded_at: ISO timestamp
bonded_by: entity who created it

# Execution state
status: running | paused | completed | failed
current_step: step-id
started_at: ISO timestamp
completed_at: ISO timestamp (if done)

# Loop tracking
reset_count: 0
last_reset_at: null

# Per-step state
steps:
  inbox-check:
    status: completed | in_progress | pending
    started_at: ...
    completed_at: ...
  spawn-work:
    status: pending
  self-inspect:
    status: pending

# Variables (from formula instantiation)
variables:
  rig: gastown
  issue_id: gt-xxx
```

## Operations

- CreateState(molID, formula, variables) - Initialize state
- LoadState(molID) - Read current state  
- SaveState(molID, state) - Write state
- AdvanceStep(molID) - Mark current complete, find next
- ResetState(molID) - Reset all steps to pending

## Files

- internal/mol/state.go
- internal/mol/types.go","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T15:53:43.381634-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T16:53:11.807645-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T16:53:11.807645-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-m6r3","title":"Refactor: Extract shared getEpicChildren helper for swarm commands","description":"## Context
`getSwarmStatus` and `analyzeEpicForSwarm` in cmd/bd/swarm.go have nearly identical code for:
- Getting epic dependents via GetDependents
- Filtering to parent-child relationships
- Building childIDSet map
- Building dependency maps

## Implementation
1. Extract `getEpicChildren(ctx, store, epicID)` that returns:
   - `[]*types.Issue` - child issues
   - `map[string]bool` - child ID set
   - `map[string][]string` - dependency map (within children)
   - `error`

2. Refactor both `analyzeEpicForSwarm` and `getSwarmStatus` to use it

3. Consider defining a named interface for the store methods:
   ```go
   type SwarmStore interface {
       GetIssue(context.Context, string) (*types.Issue, error)
       GetDependents(context.Context, string) ([]*types.Issue, error)
       GetDependencyRecords(context.Context, string) ([]*types.Dependency, error)
   }
   ```

## Files
- cmd/bd/swarm.go (lines 150-248, 616-682)

## Acceptance
- No duplicated code for fetching epic children
- Both commands produce identical results as before","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-28T21:30:55.55377-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-28T21:43:50.759453-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T21:43:50.759453-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-m8ro","title":"Improve test coverage for internal/rpc (47.5% → 60%)","description":"The RPC package has only 47.5% test coverage. RPC is the communication layer for daemon operations.

Current coverage: 47.5%
Target coverage: 60%","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T20:43:09.515299-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T22:42:11.921388-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T22:42:11.921388-08:00"}
-{"id":"bd-m964","title":"Consider FTS5 for text search at scale","description":"SearchIssues uses LIKE patterns for text search which can't use indexes.

**Current query (queries.go:1475-1477):**
```sql
(title LIKE ? OR description LIKE ? OR id LIKE ?)
```

**Problem:** Full table scan on every text search. At 100K+ issues, this becomes slow.

**SQLite FTS5 solution:**
```sql
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE issues_fts USING fts5(
  id, title, description, design, notes,
  content='issues',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

-- Triggers to keep FTS in sync
CREATE TRIGGER issues_ai AFTER INSERT ON issues BEGIN
  INSERT INTO issues_fts(rowid, id, title, description, design, notes)
  VALUES (new.rowid, new.id, new.title, new.description, new.design, new.notes);
END;
-- (similar for UPDATE, DELETE)
```

**Trade-offs:**
- Database size increase (~30-50% for text content)
- Additional write overhead (trigger execution)
- Better search capabilities (ranking, phrase search)

**Decision needed:** Is full-text search a priority feature? Current LIKE search may be acceptable for most use cases.

**Benchmark first:** Measure SearchIssues at 100K scale before implementing.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:56.466121-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:56.466121-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m964","depends_on_id":"bd-h0we","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:56.466764-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

**SQLite FTS5 solution:**

-- Triggers to keep FTS in sync

 {"id":"bd-mhhb","title":"Read operations should not modify database file","description":"Read-only operations (bd list, bd ready, bd show) modify the database file. Every open sets WAL pragma, runs schema/migrations, and checkpoints on close. Need sqlite.NewReadOnly() that opens with mode=ro, skips WAL/schema/migrations, and skips checkpoint. See GH#804.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-30T16:27:13.416643-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-30T16:46:49.921614-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T16:46:49.921614-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented read-only mode for SQLite. Read commands now use NewReadOnly() which opens with mode=ro, skips WAL pragma/schema/migrations, and skips checkpoint on close."}
 {"id":"bd-mol-0qm","title":"mol-beads-release","description":"attached_args: Release v0.38.0 - execute each step in order, verify before proceeding to next

Release checklist for beads version 0.38.0.

This molecule ensures all release steps are completed properly.
Variable: 0.38.0 - target version (e.g., 0.35.0)

## Step: update-release-notes
Update cmd/bd/info.go with release notes for 0.38.0.

Add a new VersionChange entry at the top of versionChanges slice:
```go
{
    Version: "0.38.0",
    Date:    "YYYY-MM-DD",
    Changes: []string{
        "NEW: Feature description",
        "FIX: Bug fix description",
        "IMPROVED: Enhancement description",
    },
},
```

Run `git log --oneline v<previous>..HEAD` to see what changed.

## Step: update-changelog
Update CHANGELOG.md with detailed release notes.

Add a new section after [Unreleased]:
```markdown
## [0.38.0] - YYYY-MM-DD

### Added
- **Feature name** (issue-id) - Description

### Changed
- **Change description** (issue-id)

### Fixed
- **Bug fix** (issue-id) - Description
```

Sort by importance, not chronologically.
Needs: update-release-notes

## Step: bump-version
Run the version bump script.

```bash
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.38.0
```

This updates version in all files:
- cmd/bd/version.go
- .claude-plugin/*.json
- integrations/beads-mcp/pyproject.toml
- npm-package/package.json
- Hook templates

Needs: update-changelog

## Step: run-tests
Run tests and verify lint passes.

```bash
go test -short ./...
```

CI will run full lint, but fix any obvious issues first.
Needs: bump-version

## Step: commit-release
Commit the release changes.

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: bump version to v0.38.0"
```

Needs: run-tests

## Step: push-and-tag
Push commit and create release tag.

```bash
git push origin main
git tag v0.38.0
git push origin v0.38.0
```

This triggers GitHub Actions release workflow.
Needs: commit-release

## Step: wait-for-ci
Wait for GitHub Actions to complete.

Monitor: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/actions

CI will:
- Build binaries via GoReleaser
- Create GitHub Release with assets
- Publish to npm (@beads/bd)
- Publish to PyPI (beads-mcp)
- Update Homebrew tap

Wait until all jobs succeed (~5-10 min).
Needs: push-and-tag

## Step: verify-release
Verify the release is complete.

```bash
# Check GitHub release
gh release view v0.38.0

# Check Homebrew
brew update && brew info steveyegge/beads/bd

# Check npm
npm view @beads/bd version

# Check PyPI
pip index versions beads-mcp
```

Needs: wait-for-ci

## Step: update-local
Update local installations.

```bash
# Upgrade Homebrew
brew upgrade steveyegge/beads/bd

# Or install from source
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.38.0 --install

# Install MCP locally
pip install -e integrations/beads-mcp

# Restart daemons
pkill -f "bd daemon" || true
```

Verify: `bd --version` shows 0.38.0
Needs: verify-release

## Step: manual-publish
(Optional) Manual publish if CI failed.

```bash
# npm (requires npm login)
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.38.0 --publish-npm

# PyPI (requires TWINE credentials)
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.38.0 --publish-pypi

# Or both
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.38.0 --publish-all
```

Only needed if CI publishing failed.

 {"id":"bd-n3v","title":"Error committing to sync branch: failed to create worktree","description":"> bd sync --no-daemon
→ Exporting pending changes to JSONL...
→ Committing changes to sync branch 'beads-sync'...
Error committing to sync branch: failed to create worktree: failed to create worktree parent directory: mkdir /var/home/matt/dev/beads/fix-ci/.git: not a directory","notes":"**Problem Diagnosed**: The `bd sync` command was failing with "mkdir /var/home/matt/dev/beads/fix-ci/.git: not a directory" because it was being executed from the wrong directory.

**Root Cause**: The command was run from `/var/home/matt/dev/beads` (where the `fix-ci` worktree exists) instead of the main repository directory `/var/home/matt/dev/beads/main`. Since `fix-ci` is a git worktree with a `.git` file (not directory), the worktree creation logic failed when trying to create `<current_dir>/.git/beads-worktrees/<branch>`.

**Solution Verified**: Execute `bd sync` from the main repository directory:
```bash
cd main && bd sync --dry-run
```

 {"id":"bd-n4td","title":"Add warning when staleness check errors","description":"## Problem

When ensureDatabaseFresh() calls CheckStaleness() and it errors (corrupted metadata, permission issues, etc.), we silently proceed with potentially stale data.

**Location:** cmd/bd/staleness.go:27-32

**Scenarios:**
- Corrupted metadata table
- Database locked by another process  
- Permission issues reading JSONL file
- Invalid last_import_time format in DB

## Current Code

```go
isStale, err := autoimport.CheckStaleness(ctx, store, dbPath)
if err \!= nil {
    // If we can't determine staleness, allow operation to proceed
    // (better to show potentially stale data than block user)
    return nil
}
```

## Fix

```go
isStale, err := autoimport.CheckStaleness(ctx, store, dbPath)
if err \!= nil {
    fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: Could not verify database freshness: %v\n", err)
    fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Proceeding anyway. Data may be stale.\n\n")
    return nil
}
```

## Impact
Medium - users should know when staleness check fails

## Effort
Easy - 5 minutes","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-20T20:16:34.889997-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-n4td","depends_on_id":"bd-2q6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-20T20:18:20.154723-05:00","created_by":"stevey"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}

-{"id":"bd-n663","title":"bd sync: redirect triggers 'External BEADS_DIR' mode, bypasses sync.branch","description":"## Summary

When `.beads/redirect` points to a location in the same git repo, `bd sync` incorrectly triggers "External BEADS_DIR detected, using direct commit" mode, bypassing the configured `sync.branch` workflow.

## Environment

- beads version: 0.41.0
- Setup: bare repo with worktrees (Gas Town pattern)
- Redirect: `.beads/redirect` contains `../../mayor/rig/.beads`
- Config: `git config beads.sync.branch beads-sync`

## Expected Behavior

With `sync.branch = beads-sync` configured:
1. Export changes to JSONL
2. Commit to beads-sync branch (via worktree at `.git/beads-worktrees/beads-sync`)
3. Push/pull beads-sync
4. Import JSONL
5. **No commits on main or current working branch**

## Actual Behavior

```
$ bd sync
→ Exporting pending changes to JSONL...
→ External BEADS_DIR detected, using direct commit...  ← Wrong!
→ Commits to current branch (mayor-rig)
→ Tries to push to origin/main
```

The "External BEADS_DIR" detection takes precedence over sync.branch config.

## Root Cause (suspected)

In `cmd/bd/sync.go`, the redirect resolution likely marks the BEADS_DIR as "external" because the path crosses directory boundaries, even though:
1. It resolves to the same git repository
2. `sync.branch` is explicitly configured

## Suggested Fix

When determining sync mode, check:
```go
if syncBranch != "" && isInSameGitRepo(resolvedBeadsDir, cwd) {
    // Use sync.branch mode, not direct commit
    useSyncBranch = true
} else if isExternalBeadsDir {
    // Only use direct commit for truly external repos
    useDirectCommit = true
}
```

## Workaround

Created a `mayor-rig` branch that tracks origin/main so git operations succeed, but commits still land on wrong branch.

## Related

- Affects Gas Town multi-clone setups using redirects
- GH#810 was a related sync.branch issue (worktree → local copy direction)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:43:40.800174-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-30T19:43:40.800174-08:00"}
+{"id":"bd-n663","title":"bd sync: redirect triggers 'External BEADS_DIR' mode, bypasses sync.branch","description":"## Summary

When `.beads/redirect` points to a location in the same git repo, `bd sync` incorrectly triggers "External BEADS_DIR detected, using direct commit" mode, bypassing the configured `sync.branch` workflow.

## Environment

- beads version: 0.41.0
- Setup: bare repo with worktrees (Gas Town pattern)
- Redirect: `.beads/redirect` contains `../../mayor/rig/.beads`
- Config: `git config beads.sync.branch beads-sync`

## Expected Behavior

With `sync.branch = beads-sync` configured:
1. Export changes to JSONL
2. Commit to beads-sync branch (via worktree at `.git/beads-worktrees/beads-sync`)
3. Push/pull beads-sync
4. Import JSONL
5. **No commits on main or current working branch**

## Actual Behavior

```
$ bd sync
→ Exporting pending changes to JSONL...
→ External BEADS_DIR detected, using direct commit...  ← Wrong!
→ Commits to current branch (mayor-rig)
→ Tries to push to origin/main
```

The "External BEADS_DIR" detection takes precedence over sync.branch config.

## Root Cause (suspected)

In `cmd/bd/sync.go`, the redirect resolution likely marks the BEADS_DIR as "external" because the path crosses directory boundaries, even though:
1. It resolves to the same git repository
2. `sync.branch` is explicitly configured

## Suggested Fix

When determining sync mode, check:
```go
if syncBranch != "" && isInSameGitRepo(resolvedBeadsDir, cwd) {
    // Use sync.branch mode, not direct commit
    useSyncBranch = true
} else if isExternalBeadsDir {
    // Only use direct commit for truly external repos
    useDirectCommit = true
}
```

## Workaround

Created a `mayor-rig` branch that tracks origin/main so git operations succeed, but commits still land on wrong branch.

## Related

- Affects Gas Town multi-clone setups using redirects
- GH#810 was a related sync.branch issue (worktree → local copy direction)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:43:40.800174-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-30T21:04:42.557555-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T21:04:42.557555-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed by adding hasSyncBranchConfig check to isExternal condition.  {"id":"bd-n6fm","title":"witness Handoff","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T04:35:02.675024-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T17:38:31.554705-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-n777","title":"Timer beads for scheduled agent callbacks","description":"## Problem

Agents frequently need to wait for external events (CI completion, PR reviews, artifact builds) but have no good mechanism:
- `sleep N` blocks and is unreliable (often times out at 8+ minutes)
- Polling wastes context and is easy to forget
- No way to survive session restarts

## Proposal: Timer Beads

A new bead type or field that represents a scheduled callback:

### Creating timers
```bash
bd timer create --in 30s --callback "Check CI run 12345" --issue bd-xyz
bd timer create --at "2025-12-20T08:00:00" --callback "Morning standup"
bd timer create --in 5m --on-expire "tmux send-keys -t dave 'bd show bd-xyz'"
```

### Timer storage
- Store in beads (survives restarts)
- Fields: `expires_at`, `callback_description`, `on_expire_command`, `linked_issue`
- Status: pending, fired, cancelled

### Deacon integration
The Deacon daemon monitors timer beads:
1. Wakes on next timer expiry
2. Executes `on_expire` command (e.g., tmux send-keys to interrupt agent)
3. Marks timer as fired
4. Optionally updates linked issue

### Use cases
- CI monitoring: "ping me when build completes"
- PR reviews: "check back in 1 hour"
- Scheduled tasks: "remind me at EOD to sync"
- Blocking waits: agent registers callback instead of sleeping

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Timer bead type or field design
- [ ] `bd timer create/list/cancel` commands
- [ ] Deacon timer monitoring loop
- [ ] tmux integration for agent interrupts
- [ ] Survives daemon restarts","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-19T23:05:33.051861-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:19:48.087482-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:19:48.087482-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-n97g","title":"bump-version.sh should detect pre-staged release notes","description":"When running the release workflow, I staged CHANGELOG.md and info.go changes before running bump-version.sh. The script:

1. Warned about uncommitted changes (correct)
2. Required stash/pop to proceed
3. After pop, the [Unreleased] header wasn't converted to version/date

The script could be smarter:
- Detect if staged changes are to CHANGELOG.md/info.go (expected for releases)
- Or provide a --allow-staged flag
- Or at minimum, re-apply the [Unreleased] → [X.Y.Z] transformation after detecting manual edits

Workaround: manually edit [Unreleased] to [0.39.1] - 2025-12-27 after stash pop.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-27T22:51:23.239642-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-27T23:36:47.949197-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-27T23:36:47.949197-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-rgyd","title":"Split internal/storage/sqlite/queries.go (1586 lines)","description":"Split internal/storage/sqlite/queries.go (1704 lines) into logical modules.

## Current State
queries.go is 1704 lines with mixed responsibilities:
- Issue CRUD operations
- Search/filter operations
- Delete operations (complex cascade logic)
- Helper functions (parsing, formatting)

## Proposed Split

### 1. queries.go (keep ~400 lines) - Core CRUD
```go
// Core issue operations
func (s *SQLiteStorage) CreateIssue(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) GetIssue(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) UpdateIssue(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) CloseIssue(...)
```

### 2. queries_search.go (~300 lines) - Search/Filter
```go
// Search and filtering
func (s *SQLiteStorage) SearchIssues(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) GetIssueByExternalRef(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) GetCloseReason(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) GetCloseReasonsForIssues(...)
```

### 3. queries_delete.go (~400 lines) - Delete Operations
```go
// Delete operations with cascade logic
func (s *SQLiteStorage) CreateTombstone(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) DeleteIssue(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) DeleteIssues(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) resolveDeleteSet(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) expandWithDependents(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) validateNoDependents(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) checkSingleIssueValidation(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) trackOrphanedIssues(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) collectOrphansForID(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) populateDeleteStats(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) executeDelete(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) findAllDependentsRecursive(...)
```

### 4. queries_helpers.go (~100 lines) - Utilities
```go
// Helper functions (already at top of file)
func parseNullableTimeString(...)
func parseJSONStringArray(...)
func formatJSONStringArray(...)
```

### 5. queries_rename.go (~100 lines) - ID/Prefix Operations
```go
// ID and prefix management
func (s *SQLiteStorage) UpdateIssueID(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) RenameDependencyPrefix(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) RenameCounterPrefix(...)
func (s *SQLiteStorage) ResetCounter(...)
```

## Implementation Steps

1. **Create new files** with package declaration:
   ```go
   // queries_delete.go
   package sqlite
   
   import (...)
   ```

2. **Move functions** - cut/paste, maintaining order within each file

3. **Update imports** - each file needs its own imports

4. **Run tests** after each file split:
   ```bash
   go test ./internal/storage/sqlite/...
   ```

5. **Run linter** to catch any issues:
   ```bash
   golangci-lint run ./internal/storage/sqlite/...
   ```

## File Organization
```
internal/storage/sqlite/
├── queries.go          # Core CRUD (~400 lines)
├── queries_search.go   # Search/filter (~300 lines)
├── queries_delete.go   # Delete cascade (~400 lines)
├── queries_helpers.go  # Utilities (~100 lines)
└── queries_rename.go   # ID operations (~100 lines)
```

## Success Criteria
- No file > 500 lines
- All tests pass
- No functionality changes
- Clear separation of concerns","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:17:23.85869-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:40:51.62551-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:40:51.62551-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rgyd","depends_on_id":"bd-iz5t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:44:07.50733-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-sal9","title":"bd mol current: soft cursor showing current/next step","description":"Add bd mol current command for molecule navigation orientation.

## Usage

bd mol current [mol-id]

If mol-id given, show status for that molecule.
If not given, infer from in_progress issues assigned to current agent.

## Output

You're working on molecule gt-abc (Feature X)

  [done] gt-abc.1: Design
  [done] gt-abc.2: Scaffold  
  [done] gt-abc.3: Implement
  [current] gt-abc.4: Write tests [in_progress] <- YOU ARE HERE
  [pending] gt-abc.5: Documentation
  [pending] gt-abc.6: Exit decision

Progress: 3/6 steps complete

## Key behaviors
- Shows full molecule structure with status indicators
- Highlights current in_progress step
- If no in_progress, highlights first ready step
- Works without explicit cursor tracking (inferred from state)

## Implementation notes
- Query children of mol-id
- Sort by dependency order
- Find first in_progress or first ready
- Format with status indicators

## Gas Town integration
- gt-lz13: Update templates with nav workflow
- gt-um6q: Update docs with nav workflow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T17:03:30.245964-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T17:36:31.936007-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T17:36:31.936007-08:00"}
+{"id":"bd-sco6","title":"Emit issue title with dep/label mutation events","description":"When dependencies or labels are added, mutations are emitted without title/assignee:

```go
s.emitMutation(MutationUpdate, depArgs.FromID, "", "")  // server_labels_deps_comments.go:68
s.emitMutation(MutationUpdate, issueID, "", "")         // server_labels_deps_comments.go:101
```

This causes bd activity (and gt feed) to show less informative entries like:
```
[20:42:17] → gt-4ntnq updated
```

Instead of:
```
[20:42:17] → gt-4ntnq updated · Pipeline Reliability: Fix work loss...
```

**Fix**: Look up issue title in dep/label handlers before emitting mutation. This adds one extra DB read per dep/label operation, but these are infrequent enough that it's acceptable.

**Related**: gt feed now deduplicates rapid updates in gastown commit (to be pushed).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-30T21:13:31.574249-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-30T21:21:45.081602-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T21:21:45.081602-08:00","close_reason":"Added lookupIssueMeta helper; mutation events now include title/assignee for dep/label/comment operations"}

 {"id":"bd-x3hi","title":"Support redirect files in .beads/ directory","description":"Gas Town creates polecat worktrees with .beads/redirect files that point to a shared beads database. The bd CLI should:

1. When finding a .beads/ directory, check if it contains a 'redirect' file
2. If redirect exists, read the relative path and use that as the beads directory
3. This allows multiple git worktrees to share a single beads database

Example:
- polecats/alpha/.beads/redirect contains '../../mayor/rig/.beads'
- bd commands from alpha should use mayor/rig/.beads

Currently bd ignores redirect files and either uses the local .beads/ or walks up to find a parent .beads/.

Related: gt-nriy (test message that can't be retrieved due to missing redirect support)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T21:46:23.415172-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T21:59:25.759664-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T21:59:25.759664-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-yuxq","title":"Code smell: 262 uses of interface{} reduce type safety","description":"Heavy use of interface{} across 92 files (262 occurrences) in:
- RPC update arguments: updatesFromArgs() returns map[string]interface{}
- JSON unmarshaling and formatting
- Storage query helpers

**Example in internal/rpc/server_issues_epics.go:45:**
```go
func updatesFromArgs(a UpdateArgs) map[string]interface{} {
    // Returns map with interface{} values - types must be asserted at use sites
}
```

**Problem:**
- Loss of type safety
- Runtime type assertions required
- Harder to catch errors at compile time

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Define typed update structs for specific field groups
- [ ] Use generics where possible (Go 1.18+)
- [ ] Reduce interface{} count by at least 50%
- [ ] Document expected types where interface{} remains necessary
- [ ] Tests pass","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:01.896147-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:44:43.347739-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T07:04:41.376731-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented type safety improvements:

1. Created IssueUpdate struct with typed fields and ToMap() for backward compatibility
2. Created 15+ typed response structs (StatusMessage, DeleteResult, CompactResult, etc.)
3. Refactored updatesFromArgs to use typed IssueUpdate internally
4. Updated RPC delete handler to use typed DeleteResult
5. Updated migrate.go to use StatusMessage for errors

The typed structs provide compile-time checking and reduce runtime type assertions. This foundation enables further cleanup of interface{} usage over time.

Note: Many remaining interface{} usages are inherent to Go's JSON handling and external APIs (GraphQL, SQL). The acceptance criteria of 50% reduction applies to the refactorable patterns, not the total count.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yuxq","depends_on_id":"bd-ox1o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:23.812412-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-z4f5","title":"--parent flag reports success but doesn't persist to JSONL","description":"The --parent flag for bd update reports success but the change doesn't persist.

Reported by: Mayor
Location: show.go:810-814 handles the logic

Symptoms:
- bd update <id> --parent=<new-parent> returns success
- But the parent change is not written to JSONL
- Subsequent bd show reveals parent unchanged

This shipped in v0.39.1 as part of bd-cj2e (--parent flag for reparenting).

Investigation: Check the update path from show.go through to JSONL export. The RPC/daemon layer may be dropping the parent field, or the storage layer isn't persisting it.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-27T23:10:22.428136-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-27T23:27:45.716572-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-27T23:27:45.716572-08:00"}
